name: King's Cake II
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first four ingredients in a saucepan and heat until the butter melts, stirring occasionally.
2. Let the mixture cool to 105-115 degrees.
3. Dissolve yeast and 1 tablespoon sugar in warm water in a large bowl; let stand for 5 minutes.
4. Add the butter mixture, eggs, and 2 cups flour to the yeast mixture. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer for 2 minutes or until smooth.
5. Gradually stir in enough remaining flour to make a soft dough.
6. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface, and knead until smooth and elastic (about 10 minutes).
7. Place the dough in a well-greased bowl, turning to grease the top.
8. Cover, and let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, for 1 hour, or until the dough is doubled in bulk.
9. Combine 1/2 cup sugar and cinnamon; set aside.
10. Punch down the dough, and divide it in half. Turn one portion of dough out onto a lightly floured surface, and roll to a 28x10-inch rectangle.
11. Spread half each of butter and cinnamon mixture on the dough.
12. Roll the dough, jellyroll fashion, starting at the long side.
13. Gently place the dough roll, seam side down, on a lightly greased baking sheet.
14. Bring the ends of the dough together to form an oval ring, moistening and pinching the edges together to seal.
15. Cover and let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, for 20 minutes, or until doubled in bulk.
16. Bake at 375 degrees for 20-25 minutes.
17. Repeat the procedure with the remaining dough, butter, and cinnamon mixture.
18. Decorate each cake with bands of colored frostings, and sprinkle with colored sugars.
Yield: 2 cakes.
